was out of office, but I caught up on everything that you talked about during the meeting.

So I think, you know, one of the biggest takeaways from last week was that we want to take another look at your top of the funnel content and see where are the gaps and where we can cover.
Cover, like,
Provide more coverage.
So what I did was I pulled all of the URLs from your site, from your blog, so the learn pages, and also the AI glossary.
Then we analyzed it to really see like how much of it was truly top of the funnel and middle and bottom of the funnel.
And while there is some coverage there, a lot of like the top of the funnel content isn't related to your product.
Like there are, you know, super interesting articles about like the impact of AI on climate change and things like that.
But a lot of the content also, you know, there are a lot of content gaps there.
So we took that information and went and did manual keyword research just to understand like, okay, where could you be ranking that you aren't ranking?
And I also did a very kind of traditional keyword gap analysis in SEMrush with your top competitors like Eleven Labs.
let's Bye.
to find additional opportunities where you could be ranking.
So that uncovered also additional opportunities that we added to Airtable.
So now we've labeled these two additional clusters as top of the funnel.
So one just focuses on like the core voice AI APIs.
And the other one focuses more on the architecture and the intelligence behind voice AI.
For example, like what is spoken language understanding, low latency voice AI, accent detection voice AI.
So like super, very like basic explainers.
Also batched AI transcription.
And these feed nicely into like the other content that we'll be creating that's a little further down the funnel.
And then another thing that I wanted to point out is that while we...

We, you know, obviously we want you to rank for a speech-to-text API and, you know, looking at, for example, SEMrush data right now, you are in position seven and you're ranking with a blog post, actually, not this landing page.
And ideally, this landing page would actually be at the top of the SERP here.
So I think we can support this product page with, like, a lot of internal links, mentioning this product specifically as many times as possible.
I mean, you know, within reason, but just to give it a little bit of a boost.
But I think, you know, in instances like this one, getting a product page like this to rank in position one isn't hard because the search intent is already there.
Like, the search intent is already pretty much commercial.
Like, Google's speech-to-text is ranking in position one.
So I think you do have, like, a good...
The chances are good that you're also going to be ranking for this with this product page.
Yeah, so let me see if I missed anything.
That last thing you showed us was speech-to-text or speech-to-text API?
Speech-to-text API.
Okay, all right, excellent.
What about speech-to-text just while we're here?
Like, what are the chances we can rank here as well?
This is super hard.
Yeah, super hard.

I think, like, 11 labs is ranking position 10, so I think it's worth, like, it's worth shooting for this as well.
Yeah, in terms of speech-to-text, they're no better than us, so we should definitely be ahead of them.
So I think let's shoot for this too then.
Yeah, yeah, I mean, we don't, I mean, we...
Yeah.
Yeah.
I've written landing pages before.
I've done landing pages before.

Our workflows right now, they're not optimized for landing pages, but I think we can definitely, if you do end up rewriting them or redoing them, I would be happy to kind of give my input just for the SEO portion of it.
I think that we can help out in that way.
We don't think we could rank this landing page for speech-to-text search?

I think I could audit it and see what's missing, like, because I'm sure that there are some things that we could add that searchers are looking for to kind of boost the rankings, but I know that editing landing pages can be a struggle depending on the CMS that you're using and your dev team, so I can do an audit of this page and tell you what I think.
Yeah, because I think our goal would be to rank in speech-to-text API would be the first thing, and then speech-to-text would also be a goal.
But I'd only be
Because like a lot of people even internally search speech to text and they're like, why aren't we here?
Right?
So I do the visible search.
Yeah, yeah, absolutely.
And I mean, this is something that, you I've been working in content agencies since 2021.
And I think one of the first things that I hear when I start working with a new client is we want to be ranking on the first position for our product.
That's like the baseline that people want to see.
So I totally, totally understand that.
So I think with internal linking, we can give it a boost.

And I will also take a look and see what we can do, know, try to make some recommendations to see, you know, if we can like maybe tweak some of the language on the landing page to make it a little more optimized to this.
And also, what I wanted to talk to you about, and this is something that's a bit, you know, part of, it's a broader SEO issue.
But for example, know,

When I go and check out your site in SEMrush, obviously you experienced a hit in organic rankings in August last year, and I'm aware that you have a lot of pages on your site that are no longer ranking very well.

And something that we recommend, especially in cases like this, is to try and do a pruning of the sites or really see what's no longer getting traffic, what's no longer useful, because a lot of times, you know, you will, we will remove a lot of pages and then it gets a lift in rankings because the crawl budget is bigger and it's just makes your website structure better.
I mean, Jacob, who's, who was my, who's a managing director at growthx.
He was my former manager.
He was working at G2.
And I think one of the things that he did not, maybe not a G2, but a prior employer was he came.
And the first thing that he did was remove, like, I think 200 or 300 pages that were no longer getting traffic.
And they experienced a pretty big lift in traffic just from that.
So that would be a part of, like, a bigger content audit that we can do for you.
But it would take a little bit more time because it requires us to pull, like, data from Google Search Console and SEMrush and kind of merge it all together.
Okay.
Okay.

just want to look at this graph and complete.

okay.

specific.

And then, I think, just to give you an idea of the scale, so our goal is to get up to around five blogs per week once we ramp up.

I can't, I mean, it's pretty, the actual execution, like once we develop, so what we have to do is we have to go to Atlas.
Atlas, which the content generation platform that we have, and we have to build a custom pipeline for you.

And all this.
it's like we have a lot of content.

Blog posts as well as the programmatic web page.

Yeah, I mean, for the keywords that we're targeting, it can take anywhere from two to three weeks to about a month.
To maybe a couple of, like, I mean, to see, like, overall impact, it takes three to four months minimum, I think, to see, like, the impact of the overall website.
But initial rankings can be pretty quick.
So I've published content for clients, and, you know, they start ranking within, like, two weeks on the first page for that target keyword.
So that's, it really depends on what we're targeting.
But, yeah, the cumulative results will be...
Probably four to five months.
Okay.
Okay.
So in four to five months, like if you look at our organic traffic now, which was like 100,000 over the month, what do you think we could get that up to in four months?
Like, what do you think our organic traffic would look like in four months from now?
That's a tough to answer for multiple reasons.
One is, for example, one blog post, it ranks for, we target one keyword, but it ranks for multiple keywords.
And the traffic, so the traffic is not just from that search volume, but it's also like all of these other related keywords that we don't have any insight into.
And two, and then there's also the impact of AI searches.
So there could be like some keywords where AI overviews might eat into some of the clicks.
But what I can tell you, I can go back to this.
Team and see what is like the average lift that we're seeing with clients, like the percentage month over month, that could be maybe helpful for you to kind of gauge.
What we don't want to see is the clicks going down.
So that's what we want to avoid.

I think that would probably be the most more accurate.

Is there a way we can accelerate the programmatic by providing you some example wireframes and stuff like that for the content on language pages?
Okay, so that's something I'll get with Patricia and our team to hopefully, so that you're not starting from zero, you have like an idea and then just replicate that across various languages.
Yeah, yeah, yeah, that makes sense.
All right, I think, I think that's it.
Did you have anything else that you wanted to discuss today?

No, there's something that you said struck me, which is like, especially when I look at this graph, the top organic traffic graph, and I'm saying this for the benefit of my team as much as anything else, which is like, normally the process to increase organic traffic is to create more pages, is to create more content.
That's like, 101, how do you get more organic traffic, create more content?

We're in the situation where we might actually need to create, we might need to prune our content, not create, like we're at the phase where actually what we need to do to get SEO is to reduce the number of web pages we have, not increase.

And it's such an odd situation to be in, because like, we can't just create new content, we can't just spend our energy in there, we also have to spend our energy reducing irrelevant content, so to speak.
Yeah.
